subject: Project Management
title: The Overconfidence Effect
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Overconfidence Effect.

hook.text: Ask people for a 90% confidence interval on a question. They're wrong about 50% of the time.

buildup.text: Overconfidence means we're more certain of our estimates than the evidence justifies.

discovery.text: Experts are often more overconfident than novices — their expertise breeds unwarranted certainty.

twist.text: In project estimation, overconfidence narrows our ranges. We say '3 months' when we should say '3 to 9.'

climax.text: Overconfidence isn't arrogance — it's a cognitive illusion. We genuinely believe our narrow estimates.

punchline.text: The more sure you are, the wider your range should be.

quiz.question: How does overconfidence affect project estimates?
options: It makes our ranges too narrow — we're more certain than we should be
It makes us give up too early
It only affects junior team members
